summary.intent: Enhance the Gossamer app with two features: (1) UI indication when Claude thinking blocks are redacted, and (2) a branch selector dropdown that lets users switch branches and view associated checkpoints with commit messages.
summary.outcome: Both features were fully implemented: redacted thinking now shows a dimmed 'thinking (redacted)' indicator in ClaudeTurnCard; a branch selector lives in the Layout header bar on the /branch-log route; checkpoints display commit messages as fallback intent text and include commit hashes; and several CheckpointRow layout tweaks were made (removed green border, reordered elements, removed 'Checkpoint' label and 'Open checkpoint' link).

repo: The indexer already stores redacted_thinking blocks with contentType='redacted_thinking' and thinking=null — the UI just wasn't detecting them.
CheckpointRow is defined in SessionDetail.tsx and imported by BranchLog.tsx — one component definition serves both views.
Branch-log data flows: fetchBranchLog → BranchLogEntry → entryToCheckpoint → SessionCheckpoint → CheckpointRow.
The /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint is session-scoped and does not have commit context, so commitMessage and commitHash are null there.
git log format string uses %H%x00%B%x1e to capture hash and body together; %x1e is the record separator used to delimit entries.

code.finding: CheckpointRow fallback rendering: shows sum?.intent if present, else checkpoint.commitMessage in monospace dimmed text.
BranchSelector component renders in the header only when pathname === '/branch-log', reads localPath/branch from search params, fetches branches on mount.
GET /api/branches endpoint runs 'git -C <path> branch' and strips the leading '* ' from the current branch entry.
EntryDetail calls CheckpointRow with result of entryToCheckpoint(entry), which maps all BranchLogEntry fields including commitMessage and commitHash.

workflow: Lazy-loading state (fetching on dropdown open) causes re-renders that close the dropdown mid-interaction — eager fetch on mount is the correct pattern for dropdown data.
TypeScript build checks (tsc) were used after each significant change to catch type errors early rather than at runtime.
When a feature ends up on the wrong screen, it's cleaner to revert the wrong file fully and re-implement in the correct one rather than moving code incrementally.

friction: Branch dropdown was initially placed on ActiveSessions screen instead of Repos screen, requiring a revert and re-implementation.

The branch dropdown was then moved again from Repos.tsx to Layout.tsx after the user clarified it should be in the top bar header and only visible when inside an individual repo — two rounds of misplacement.
Lazy dropdown loading caused the dropdown to close itself when setBranches() fired during open state, requiring a switch to eager mount-time fetching.
open_items: The app needs to be rebuilt for changes to appear in the running Electron window — no hot-reload confirmation was mentioned.

The /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint returns commitMessage: null and commitHash: null since it has no git context — if commit info is ever needed in session-scoped views, that endpoint would need enhancement.
The 'Open checkpoint' link was removed because it doesn't do anything — checkpoint restore/navigation functionality is unimplemented.
